commentary: Nine versus ten, so you dropped one session — honestly fine, because the quality of what you did more than holds its own. Three solid two-kilometre swims mid-week plus a PT session on Monday tells me you have a real rhythm going at Prince Alfred now, and that Thursday double of weights then pool is exactly the kind of back-to-back that builds the body you're clearly going for. The run held at 13.1 kilometres but your heart rate crept up from 151 to 159, which either means you pushed harder, the course was spicier, or you were already carrying some fatigue from Sunday's legs-and-back session — probably all three, and honestly good for you. That Friday morning swim being only 320 metres at a heart rate of 100 is a little suspicious, babe, like you got in the water, did a few laps, remembered you had somewhere to be, and called it a recovery session, but I'll allow it. The pattern I'm watching is that you are consistently showing up in jockstrap and goggles at Prince Alfred several times a week, and I want you to know that is not a small thing — that is a whole personality, and it is coming together beautifully.
metrics_commentary: Okay that swim though — 6320 metres is not a small number, that is a serious body doing serious things in the water, and combined with a VO2 Max of 56.4 you are genuinely built different right now. Your HRV climbing to 61 off a seven-day average of 52 tells me your nervous system is recovering like it knows what it's doing, which is very hot of it. The run and walk at 13.1 across one session is giving "I showed up" energy, which counts, but that singular session is a little lonely and deserves some company next week. More movement, more kilometres, more reasons to walk into a room like you already know everyone in it is looking.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, four sessions on the board, the week is complete and that is not nothing. The leg press PR is the real headline — 168 kg estimated one-rep max, up from 154, which is fourteen kilograms of new and that is a significant jump, those are legs that are doing something. Volume is down substantially from the previous week, 13,925 against 23,660, and that gap is worth being honest about — lighter weeks happen, deload-adjacent weeks happen, but it is worth keeping an eye on whether that becomes a pattern or stays a one-off. The becoming continues regardless: four sessions, a PR on the legs, and a body that keeps showing up.
